name: Sea Bass with Peppers and Pumpkin Seed Salsa
recipeIngredient: 1 cup fresh or frozen corn kernels
1 poblano chile, roasted, peeled, seeded, and chopped
6 Chilean sea bass fillets (6-8 oz each)
1 red bell pepper, roasted, peeled, seeded, and cut into strips
recipeInstructions:
1. For the salsa, combine the corn, tomatillos, pumpkin seeds, and poblano chile.
2. Season with the lime juice, cumin, salt, and pepper. Set aside in the refrigerator for 2 hours if time permits.
3. Season the fish lightly with salt and pepper.
4. Grill, broil, or bake the fish until done to your taste (8 minutes per inch of thickness).
5. Serve with the salsa and garnish with roasted pepper strips.
Notes:
This salsa is best if prepared about 2 hours ahead. It will keep overnight but may have a slightly softer texture. This sauce works well with grilled, broiled, or baked fish; use whichever cooking method you prefer. The fish is slightly underdone but not rare; adjust based on your preference.
